{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks about the contractile part of intrafusal fibers. Intrafusal fibers are found within muscle spindles and are involved in proprioception. According to the retrieved documents, particularly Document [21] and Document [13], the central portion of the intrafusal fiber does not have myofibrils and is non-contractile. However, the ends of the intrafusal fibers are described as being innervated by gamma motor neurons and capable of contracting in response to feedback. Document [21] states: 'The central portion of a muscle spindle does not have myofibrils and does not have contracting capabilities; however, the ends of these spindles do contract in response to gamma motor neurons.' This indicates that the contractile part is located at the ends, not the center. Therefore, the contractile part is at the ends. Option B correctly identifies this.", "answer_choice": "B"}
